Update to the Wires Part
We updated the Country Specific Guidelines and Instructions, Form 3597, and the International Bank Account Number (IBAN) Countries, Form 9252, as described below. We also added a new Purpose of Payment and Category Codes form for the related Central African countries.

Central 1âs correspondent bank provided the following regulatory information:
âXAF is the currency of the Economic and Monetary Community of Central Africa (CEMAC), governed by the Banque des Etats de l'Afrique Centrale (Bank of the Central African States, BEAC) which comprises Cameroon, the Central African Republic, Chad, the Republic of the Congo, Equatorial Guinea, and Gabon.
The above-mentioned countries have begun accepting IBANs in payment instructions. The IBAN consists of 27 alphanumeric characters, composed of 4 characters country code and a 23-digit RelevÃ© d'IdentitÃ© Bancaire (RIB â Bank Account identifier).
Sample: CM21 13001 00001 10XXXXXXXX5 52 (e.g. Cameroon)â.
